classification.crosswalk_note: Factors a fourth-power difference before evaluating the limit.
classification.framework: AP Calculus Course and Exam Description, current Unit 1 topic framework

questions.prompt_html: If  = -32, find k.
questions.prompt_text: If (x →k) is under (lim) ((x) with superscript (4) -( k) with superscript (4)/x-k) = -32, find k.
questions.solution_html:
First observe that x4 - k4 = (x2 - k2 )(x2 + k2 ) = (x - k)(x + k)(x2 + k2 )
Hence, =  = (x + k)(x2 + k2 ) = (2k)(2k2) = 4k3
Now 4k3 = -32 ⇒ k3 = -8, therefore k =  = -2.
questions.solution_text: First observe that x 4 - k 4 = (x 2 - k 2 )(x 2 + k 2 ) = (x - k)(x + k)(x 2 + k 2 ) Hence, (x →k) is under (lim) ((x) with superscript (4) -( k) with superscript (4)/x - k) = (x →k) is under (lim) ((x - k)(x + k)((x) with superscript (2) +( k) with superscript (2) )/x - k) = (x →k) is under (lim) (x + k)(x 2 + k 2 ) = (2k)(2k 2 ) = 4k 3 Now 4k 3 = -32 ⇒ k 3 = -8, therefore k = (-8) to the (3) root = -2.

feedback_html.solution:
First observe that x4 - k4 = (x2 - k2 )(x2 + k2 ) = (x - k)(x + k)(x2 + k2 )
Hence, =  = (x + k)(x2 + k2 ) = (2k)(2k2) = 4k3
Now 4k3 = -32 ⇒ k3 = -8, therefore k =  = -2.

images.alt: ((x) with superscript (4) -( k) with superscript (4)/x - k)
